title: February 23 TURN: Rhyme and Reason – Preparing for Ash Wednesday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: February 23, 2020

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on the Last Sunday after Epiphany (also called Transfiguration Sunday), falling exactly one week before Ash Wednesday on March 4, 2020. The RCL readings for this date would have been 2 Kings 2:1-12 (Elijah's transfiguration), 2 Corinthians 4:3-6, and Mark 9:2-9 (the Transfiguration narrative). This positioned the sermon at the liturgical threshold between Ordinary Time and Lent, making the theme of "Preparing for Ash Wednesday" particularly resonant with the church calendar's transition.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a relatively stable period denominationally, with no major General Convention actions imminent (the next triennial would be in July 2021). Bishop Kee Sloan was serving the Diocese of Colorado at this time, having been consecrated in 2017. Saint John's Cathedral itself was in its historic downtown location and serving as the spiritual center of the diocese without major internal controversies in the immediate news cycle.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical late winter conditions with mild February weather leading into spring. The Colorado legislature was in active session (the 2020 session ran January 8-May 6), so state political news would have been locally prominent. No major weather events or community crises dominated Denver headlines in the immediate week before this date.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The COVID-19 pandemic was beginning to enter American awareness, with the first confirmed case in Colorado announced on March 5 (one day after this sermon), though early February still saw it as primarily a foreign concern. The 2020 Democratic primary was heating up with Super Tuesday scheduled for March 3, just nine days away, making Bernie Sanders and Joe Biden the dominant national political story. The Trump administration was in the midst of the second impeachment trial proceedings which had concluded with acquittal on February 5.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The COVID-19 outbreak was rapidly worsening in China and beginning to spread in Italy and Iran, though Western media coverage was still limited and the scale of the coming pandemic was not yet apparent. The U.S.-Iran tensions that had peaked in early January with Soleimani's assassination had cooled considerably by late February. Democratic protests and instability continued in Hong Kong following the anti-extradition movement, though without the intensity of the previous summer's demonstrations.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was operating in what would be the last weeks of pre-pandemic normalcy, with no sense of the approaching social disruption. The cultural moment was characterized by partisan political intensity surrounding the presidential election cycle, with anxiety about the general election seven months away. Sports were proceeding normally with the NBA and college basketball seasons in full swing, and the Denver Nuggets were having a solid regular season heading toward March.
